title: Implement spline/curve editor for keyframes
description:
Add a spline/curve editor UI for fine-tuning keyframe interpolation. This includes:

- Bezier curve editor component (similar to After Effects graph editor)
- Visual representation of property values over time
- Draggable control points for bezier handles
- Easing presets (ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out, linear, custom)
- Integration with keyframe system to edit interpolation curves
- Zoom/pan controls for timeline navigation

Reference: subformer spline editor at /Users/mohebifar/dev/other/kareem/subformer

title: Make menu bar functional
description: The menu bar is currently fully decorative/non-functional. Wire up all menu items to their corresponding actions (File: new project, open, save, export; Edit: undo, redo, cut, copy, paste, delete; View: zoom controls, panel toggles; etc). Menu items without implementations should be disabled/grayed out.

title: Add audio effects (EQ, reverb, compression, noise gate)
description:
Add audio processing effects beyond volume and speed. Potential effects to implement:

- **EQ (Equalizer)**: Low/mid/high band adjustment for tonal control
- **Reverb**: Room simulation for spatial depth
- **Compression**: Dynamic range compression to even out volume levels
- **Noise gate**: Suppress audio below a threshold to remove background noise
- **Low-pass / High-pass filters**: Frequency filtering

Each effect needs:
1. Rust implementation in crates/audio-engine (DSP processing in the mixer pipeline)
2. UI controls in the audio properties panel (apps/ui/src/components/editor/audio-properties.tsx)
3. Keyframeable parameters via the existing KeyframeInput system
4. Timeline state serialization (AudioClipState in packages/render-engine/src/audio-engine.ts)
5. Support in both playback (AudioWorklet) and export (offline WASM rendering) paths
close_reason: Fully implemented: EQ with visual frequency response editor, compressor, noise gate, and reverb all have WASM DSP + UI controls + keyframe support

title: Transition system: in, out, and cross transitions
description: Add ability to apply transitions (fade, dissolve, wipe, slide, etc.) to clips. Transitions should be droppable from the left panel onto clip edges (in/out) or between adjacent clips (cross transitions). The left panel should show a visual preview of each transition type. Leverage the existing transition types already defined in the render engine.

title: Add project settings (dimensions, resolution, orientation, frame rate)
description: Add a project settings UI that allows users to configure: video dimensions (width/height), resolution presets (1080p, 4K, etc), orientation (landscape/portrait), and frame rate (24, 30, 60 fps, custom). Settings should be stored in the project record in DexieJS and applied to the compositor/export pipeline.

title: Timeline markers with in/out points
description:
## Summary
Add named timeline markers and in/out point system for marking regions of interest.

## UX Design

### Marker creation
- M: Drop a marker at the current playhead position
- Marker appears as a small colored triangle on the ruler area (above tracks, below timecode)
- Default color: cyan. Can be changed per marker.
- Double-click marker triangle to rename (inline text edit on ruler)
- Right-click marker: context menu with Rename, Change Color, Delete

### Marker navigation
- Shift+M: Go to next marker
- Alt+M (or Ctrl+Shift+M): Go to previous marker
- Markers listed in a dropdown/popover accessible from the toolbar (optional, lower priority)

### In/Out points
- I: Set in-point at playhead
- O: Set out-point at playhead
- In/out displayed as a highlighted region on the ruler (semi-transparent overlay between in and out)
- Alt+I / Alt+O: Clear in/out point
- In/out points constrain export range when set (pass to export dialog)
- In/out points constrain playback loop when loop mode is enabled

### Visual design
- Markers: small downward-pointing triangles (6px) on the ruler, same row as timecode
- Marker label: tiny text above the triangle, visible on hover or always if space permits
- In/out region: subtle colored overlay on the ruler between the two points
- In point: [ bracket icon. Out point: ] bracket icon.

### Store changes
- Add markers: Array<{ id, time, name, color }> to project state
- Add inPoint: number | null, outPoint: number | null to store
- Actions: addMarker, removeMarker, updateMarker, setInPoint, setOutPoint, clearInOutPoints
- Markers saved with project (persisted to IndexedDB)
- In/out points are session-only (not persisted)

### Integration with export
- If in/out points are set, export dialog should show option to export only the marked region
- Default: export full timeline. Checkbox: "Export in/out range only"

title: Gesture to move clips between tracks
description: Add ability to use drag gestures to move a clip/text/shape from one track to another. Should work with linked clips (video+audio moving together to their respective track types).

title: Fix undo/redo requiring multiple keypresses
description: Bug: between every undo/redo action, the user needs to press Cmd+Z multiple times for it to take effect. The first undo works with a single Cmd+Z, but subsequent undos require pressing Cmd+Z twice (or more). This suggests the temporal store (zundo) is recording duplicate or no-op history entries, or the undo handler is consuming keystrokes without performing the undo.

title: Preview doesn't update when paused and making changes
description: When paused, moving a clip or making any change doesn't update the preview. The preview must re-render when timeline state changes while paused.

title: Linked clip doesn't move visually during drag gesture
description: When moving a clip, the linked audio/video clip doesn't move with it during the move gesture. It only moves on mouse up. The UX doesn't feel good - linked clips should move together in real-time during the drag.

title: Fix file import via menu bar (File > Import Media)
description: The File > Import Media menu item opens the file picker dialog, but after selecting a file, nothing happens — the asset is not added to the assets panel. The file picker integration (importFilesWithPicker) is not completing the import flow when triggered from the menu bar.

title: Drag and drop files from OS file explorer directly to timeline
description: Allow dragging files from Finder/file explorer directly onto the timeline to import and place them in one action. If the dropped file matches an already-imported asset (by name/size/type), reuse the existing asset rather than duplicating it in the assets panel. If it's a new file, import it as a new asset first, then add the clip to the timeline at the drop position.

title: Timeline drag-to-select (rubber band selection)
description: Add rubber-band / marquee drag selection to the timeline view. Currently multi-select only works by holding Shift/Cmd and clicking individual clips. Users should be able to click and drag on empty timeline space to draw a selection rectangle that selects all clips it intersects.

title: Add ability to add and remove tracks
description:
Implement UI and functionality to:
- Add new video/audio track pairs to the timeline
- Remove existing tracks from the timeline

title: Show video clip thumbnails in timeline
description:
Display thumbnail previews for video clips in the timeline:
- Performant rendering like subformer
- Handle zoom in/out properly (show more/fewer thumbnails)
- Reference: /Users/mohebifar/dev/other/kareem/subformer

title: Create numeric input component with drag-to-adjust
description:
Create a numeric input component that:
- Can increase/decrease value by click and drag left/right
- Allows direct editing when clicked
- Accepts a suffix prop for units (e.g., '%', 'px', '°')
- Will be used in the properties panel for transform/effect controls

title: Generate and display project thumbnails
description: Projects should have a thumbnail that is shown on the project list page. Generate a thumbnail from the project content (e.g., render a frame from the timeline at a representative time) and store it as thumbnailDataUrl in the DexieJS projects table. Display these thumbnails in the project cards on the home/projects page.

title: Proper handling of clip trimming
description:
Implement proper clip trimming functionality:
- Trim from left edge (adjusts start time and in-point)
- Trim from right edge (adjusts duration)
- Visual feedback during trim operation
- Respect asset duration limits

title: Snap clips in timeline (move and trim)
description: When moving a clip close to another clip's edge, snap them together automatically. When trimming a clip, snap to edges of clips on other tracks. Show a visual snap line indicator when snapping occurs. Should work for both move and trim operations across all tracks.

title: J/K/L playback shortcuts and expanded keyboard navigation
description:
## Summary
Add industry-standard NLE keyboard shortcuts for playback control and navigation.

## UX Design

### J/K/L Playback (highest priority)
- L: Play forward. Press again to increase speed (1x -> 2x -> 4x -> 8x)
- K: Pause. Hold K + tap L = step forward one frame. Hold K + tap J = step backward one frame.
- J: Play reverse. Press again to increase reverse speed (1x -> 2x -> 4x -> 8x)
- Speed resets when switching direction or pressing K
- Show current playback speed in the playback controls bar when not 1x (e.g. "2x" or "-4x")

### Frame-accurate navigation
- , (comma): Previous frame (already in menu, verify wired)
- . (period): Next frame (already in menu, verify wired)
- Shift+, : Jump back 1 second (10 frames at 30fps, or fps-based)
- Shift+. : Jump forward 1 second
- Home: Jump to start of timeline (verify working)
- End: Jump to end of last clip (verify working)

### Clip navigation
- Up arrow: Select previous clip on same track (or move selection up a track)
- Down arrow: Select next clip on same track (or move selection down a track)
- Shift+Left/Right: Nudge selected clip by 1 frame
- Alt+Left/Right: Nudge selected clip by 10 frames

### Implementation notes
- All shortcuts registered in the existing global keydown handler in canvas-timeline.tsx
- Skip when focus is on INPUT/TEXTAREA (existing pattern)
- Store needs: playbackSpeed state, setPlaybackSpeed action
- Audio engine needs speed parameter adjustment during J/K/L playback
- Playback controls bar should show speed indicator when not 1x

title: Implement audio renderer WASM module
description: Create an audio renderer WASM module for audio processing and playback. Use subformer's implementation at /Users/mohebifar/dev/other/kareem/subformer for inspiration on how audio rendering is handled.

title: Ripple editing mode
description:
## Summary
Add a ripple editing mode that shifts downstream clips when deleting, trimming, or moving clips.

## UX Design

### Toggle
- Ripple toggle button in TimelineToolbar (next to tool selector)
- Keyboard shortcut: R to toggle (consistent with V=Select, C=Razor pattern)
- Visual indicator: button stays highlighted when active

### Behavior when ripple is ON

**Ripple Delete:**
- Deleting a clip shifts all clips to the right on the same track left to fill the gap
- Linked clips (audio+video pairs) ripple together
- Other tracks are NOT affected

**Ripple Trim:**
- Trimming right edge: downstream clips shift to match
- Trimming left edge: clip and all downstream clips shift together
- Visual preview: ghost outlines show where downstream clips will land during drag

### Behavior when ripple is OFF
- Current behavior (no change)

### Store Changes
- Add rippleMode boolean to store state with toggleRippleMode() action
- Add rippleDelete(clipId) that removes clip and shifts downstream
- Modify trimLeft/trimRight to accept a ripple flag
- Add utility: getDownstreamClips(trackId, afterTime)

### Visual Feedback
- Subtle indicator on timeline when ripple mode is active
- During ripple drag/trim, show ghost positions of affected downstream clips

title: Audio clip fade handles on timeline
description:
## Summary
Add draggable fade-in/fade-out handles on audio clip edges in the timeline for quick volume fades.

## UX Design

### Visual design
- Small triangular handles at the top corners of audio clips on the timeline
- Fade-in handle: top-left corner. Fade-out handle: top-right corner
- Handles 8x8px, visible on hover over audio clips
- Fade region draws a curved line from 0 to 100% volume
- Fade curve rendered as a semi-transparent overlay on the clip, above the waveform

### Interaction
- Drag fade-in handle rightward to increase fade-in duration
- Drag fade-out handle leftward to increase fade-out duration
- Minimum fade: 1 frame. Maximum fade: half the clip duration
- Snapping: fade handles snap to grid lines (same as clip snap)
- Double-click a fade handle to reset it to 0 (remove fade)

### Data model
- Uses dedicated fadeIn/fadeOut duration fields on the clip (NOT keyframes)
- Fade durations are relative to clip edges so they survive trimming
- The WASM audio mixer already supports per-clip fade_in and fade_out durations
- Currently these are always 0 because there is no UI to set them

### Store changes
- Add fadeIn and fadeOut number fields to AudioClip type (duration in frames)
- Add setClipFadeIn(clipId, frames) and setClipFadeOut(clipId, frames) actions
- Pass fade values through useAudioEngine timeline sync with framesToSeconds conversion
- These are undoable (temporal-tracked state)

### Timeline rendering (Konva)
- Detect hover near top corners of audio clips (8px hit zone)
- Show ew-resize cursor on hover
- During drag, render the fade curve preview in real-time
- Fade region: filled area between the straight top edge and the curve

title: Implement video export with muxer and web worker parallelization
description: Add ability to export videos using the same muxer method and web worker parallelization used in subformer. Reference: /Users/mohebifar/dev/other/kareem/subformer

title: Project persistence with IndexedDB and project chooser page
description: Store projects in IndexedDB using DexieJS. Add a project list page (home/index route) where users can see saved projects, create new ones, and open existing ones. Projects should auto-save on changes (debounced). Store project name, settings, timeline content, and thumbnail.

title: Add shapes, texts, images, clips and audios to timeline
description:
Implement ability to add various layer types to the timeline:
- Shape layers (rectangles, circles, polygons, lines)
- Text layers
- Image layers
- Video clips
- Audio clips
close_reason: Already implemented: Asset panel, text panel, and shape panel all support drag-and-drop to timeline. Canvas timeline has drop handlers for all clip types. Store has addClipToTrack action supporting video, audio, image, text, and shape clips.

title: Auto-place first clip at timeline start
description: When adding the very first clip to an empty timeline, automatically place it at time 0 (the beginning). This only applies when the timeline has no existing clips. Subsequent clips should still be placed at the drop position or current time.

title: Add transform mode and view mode toggle for preview panel
description: Introduce two modes for the preview panel: View mode and Transform mode, with toggle buttons below the preview. Currently, clicking on clip boundaries in the preview or selecting any clip from the timeline shows transform handles (resize/move). This should only happen in Transform mode. In View mode, transform handles should be hidden and clips should not be movable/resizable in the preview. Add toggle buttons (e.g. icons) below the preview panel to switch between modes.

title: Add mute/unmute and lock/unlock for tracks
description:
Implement track controls:
- Mute/unmute: For video tracks, this hides the track's clips from rendering. For audio tracks, this mutes the audio.
- Lock/unlock: Prevents editing of clips on locked tracks

title: Show audio waveform on audio clips
description:
Display audio waveform visualization on audio clips in the timeline:
- Performant rendering like subformer
- Handle zoom in/out properly
- Reference: /Users/mohebifar/dev/other/kareem/subformer

title: Multi-select clips: batch move and trim
description: Allow selecting multiple clips in the timeline (click + shift/ctrl, or drag-select). Moving one selected clip moves all selected clips. Trimming/extending one selected clip applies the same delta to all selected clips.

title: Fix overlap handling to trim instead of delete
description:
Fix the behavior of overlap handling. When a clip overlaps another clip:
- Instead of deleting the overlapped clip, it should trim it to avoid the overlap
- Only delete if the clip is fully overlapped (100% covered)
- Partial overlaps should result in trimming the overlapped portion

title: Drag and drop assets from assets panel to preview
description: Allow dragging assets from the assets panel directly into the preview panel. The asset should be added at the current playhead timestamp on the highest (frontmost) video track. If the highest track is already occupied at the playhead position and/or adding the asset would overlap existing clips, automatically create a new track pair and place the asset there instead.

title: Implement keyframe animation system
description:
Add keyframe animation support for clip properties (position, scale, rotation, opacity, etc.). This includes:

- Keyframe data model in clip state (KeyframeTracks with property -> keyframe[] mapping)
- Keyframe interpolation using the existing EvaluatorManager from render-engine
- UI for adding/removing keyframes at current playhead position
- Visual keyframe indicators on timeline clips
- Property panel integration to show animated values

Reference: subformer implementation at /Users/mohebifar/dev/other/kareem/subformer

title: Playhead is not movable during playback
description: While playing, the user cannot move the playhead to scrub/seek. It just keeps playing and ignores playhead drag interaction. Expected: dragging the playhead during playback should pause and seek (or seek while playing).

title: Drag ghost should reflect actual clip duration
description: When dragging a clip from the assets panel onto the timeline, the preview ghost/shadow should be representative of the actual duration of the video/audio asset, not a fixed size. The ghost width should match what the clip will look like at the current zoom level.

title: Cut (Cmd+X) and Duplicate clip operations
description:
## Summary
Implement cut (copy + delete) and duplicate operations. Cut menu item exists but is disabled.

## UX Design

### Cut (Cmd+X)
- Copy selected clips to clipboard, then delete them
- If ripple mode is on (see tooscut-k4m), ripple-delete after copy
- If ripple mode is off, just delete (leave gap)
- Linked clips: cut both video and audio together
- Menu item in Edit menu already exists — enable it and wire handler

### Duplicate (Cmd+D)
- Duplicate selected clips and place them immediately after the originals
- New clips start at the end of the rightmost selected clip
- Linked clips: duplicate both video and audio
- Select the new duplicates (deselect originals)
- Useful for repeating patterns, creating variations

### Implementation
- Store actions needed: cutSelectedClips() — calls copySelectedClips() then removeClip() for each
- Store actions needed: duplicateSelectedClips() — deep-clone clips, assign new IDs, offset startTime
- Keyboard handler: add Cmd+X and Cmd+D to global keydown in canvas-timeline.tsx
- Menu: enable existing Cut item, add Duplicate item to Edit menu
- Duplicate should generate new clip IDs and new linkedClipIds for pairs

title: Make entire assets panel a dropzone for file imports
description: Make the entire assets panel act as a dropzone so users can drag and drop files from Finder/file explorer anywhere on the assets panel to import them. Currently only specific areas may accept drops.
